Many overlapping myths from different ancient places,
usually involving a woman jumping into the water,
often involving fish. Not to be confused with PISCES.
One of the Nicolas Louis de Lacaille series,
has no myths attatched to it.
A sea monster from greek myth, it's just
about as much of a whale as the Wind Fish
is a wind or a fish, which is to say, neither.
Extra note: Cetus is also a denizen known for
poisoning the seas in the Land of Light and Rain

PHOENIX - The Bird Reborn From The Ashes
PHOENIX - The Bird Reborn From The Ashes
Said to be colorful and eagle-like, Ovid claimed it
had a lifespan of 500 years, which culminated in
building a nest atop a palm tree to be set on fire.
In greek myth, it was the river that Phaëton fell
into after being struck by lightning, after
joy-riding and crashing the chariot of the sun.
Representing the goddess of justice, Dike, she was
meant to rule over human justice in the "Golden Age",
before seasons beyond spring or even death was known.
Upon the dawn of the silver age, when Zeus overthrew
Cronus, he put the seasons into motion, which humans
did not appreciate. This rising emnity made Dike turn
her back upon mankind, abandoning them in times of war.
Means "the goat" in latin, one of the zodiac
constellations. Is also associated with Pan,
He had rescued the other gods while they were at war
with the titans, once scaring monsters away with his
conch shell. Pan ran away from Typhon by jumping into
the river Nile and gaining a fish tail.
Named by Dutch explorers, sometimes called
The Flamingo. This bird was sacred to Hermes.
Before fighting against the Titans, Zeus and his
siblings swore upon this altar. After ten years, they
won the war and placed the altar among the stars.
Depicts Laelaps, the fastest dog in the world.
It once pursued an uncatchable fox and never stopped
pursuing until both were turned to stone.
Either a centaur or a satyr, this archer takes aim
at the nearby scorpion, represented by Scorpius.
One of the brightest constellations, it has many names
and legends across many cultures. In Greek myth, he
chased after women and got chased by a scorpion.
A bird sacred to Hera. She was said to drive in a
flying chariot pulled by peacocks. The tail "eyes" are
said to commemorate the many eyes of Argus the Giant.
A massive snake held by Asclepius, the healer son
Part of the former Argo Navis constellation.
The Argo was a legendary ship used by
Jason and the Argonauts to seek the Golden Fleece.
Part of the former Argo Navis constellation, it was
split into three separate constellations by a French
astronomer, likely because it was just so expansive.
Part of the former Argo Navis constellation.
It had been fitted with an oak beam by Athena that
could speak, due to being part of an oracle.
Apollo's sacred bird. In legends was originally white.
Its feathers were blackened by Apollo's curse,
cast in rage over the raven's hesitation to serve.
The offspring of Typhon and Echidna, a snake with many
heads which would multiply upon decapitation, unless
cauterized at the base. Was slain by Heracles.
With the top of a human and the bottom of a bull,
it makes you wonder how they would wear pants.
A wolf or something similar, it is impaled by the
centaur. It is held up to the altar as an offering.
Was once Scorpio's claws, it became associated with
balance due to the time of year the sun once crossed
A legendary healer who learned how to revive people
Was struck down for being too good at medicine.

A scorpion sent to sting Orion, either to protect
